source_pr.title: tests: fix unstable graceshutdown test
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: ref #36669 Problem Summary: In `connectTiDB`, when we tried 5 times to connect TiDB and all of them failed, it returned `db, nil`. The reason is `db.Close()` reset `err` to `nil`. ### What is changed and how it works? 1. Increase retry times. 2. Return error when all retries fails. ### Check List Tests - [ ] Unit test - [x] Integration test - [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility Documentation - [ ] Affects user behaviors - [ ] Contains syntax changes - [ ] Contains variable changes - [ ] Contains experimental features - [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility ### Release note Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note. ```release-note None ```

symptoms: The graceshutdown test is timing-sensitive: connecting to TiDB can take longer than the fixed retry budget, and the retry loop could accidentally swallow the last connection error.
failure_signature: connectTiDB: after exhausting retries, the function could return `db, nil` due to `db.Close()` overwriting `err`.
root_cause_explanation: The graceshutdown test relies on connecting to a TiDB instance which may not be ready within a small fixed retry budget. Previously `connectTiDB` only retried 5 times and, when all retries failed, it could still return `db, nil` because `db.Close()` overwrote the connection error with a nil close error. The patch makes the test more robust by increasing the retry budget (`maxRetry := 10`) and by using a separate `err1` for `db.Close()` so the connection error is preserved and returned when retries are exhausted.
fix_pattern: Make `connectTiDB` retry more robust: increase max retry attempts (5→10) and avoid clobbering the connection error with `db.Close()`'s error so failures are surfaced correctly.

evidence: evidence: tests: fix unstable graceshutdown test
